title: Integrative Inquiry
definition: A methodology in science that values the process of connecting different disciplines and fields to drive new discoveries.

difficulty_levels.beginner: In integrative inquiry, scientists collaborate across disciplines to find connections that might lead to groundbreaking discoveries.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Integrative inquiry encourages the exploration of synergies between seemingly unrelated fields to foster cross-pollination of ideas and accelerate scientific progress.
difficulty_levels.expert: Integrative inquiry represents a paradigm shift from linear, siloed thinking towards holistic, integrated approaches to scientific discovery.

extra_fields.insight: The most profound breakthroughs are increasingly happening at the boundaries between disciplines; integrative inquiry emphasizes this trend.
